openmeter/subscription/addon/diff.purpose: Pure in-memory spec mutation library that converts a SubscriptionAddon into a bidirectional Apply/Restore transform on SubscriptionSpec. Its sole constraint is invertibility: Apply followed by Restore must yield the original spec with no DB or service calls.

patterns.name: Diffable interface as the unit of work
patterns.description: Every spec mutation is expressed as a Diffable{GetApplies() AppliesToSpec, GetRestores() AppliesToSpec}. Both methods return an AppliesToSpec passed to spec.Apply(). Never call any repo or domain service inside Diffable implementations.
patterns.example:
diffable, err := addondiff.GetDiffableFromAddon(subView, subsAdd)
if diffable == nil { return nil } // no instances
spec.Apply(diffable.GetApplies(), subscription.ApplyContext{CurrentTime: now})

patterns.name: Gap-based item insertion in apply.go
patterns.description: apply.go tracks coverage gaps ([]timeutil.OpenPeriod) starting from the full addon cadence, subtracts each existing item's intersection, then creates new SubscriptionItemSpec for remaining gaps. Existing items that overlap are split into difference + intersection fragments.

patterns.name: Quantity application asymmetry
patterns.description: For existing items within the addon cadence, rc.Apply is called Quantity times. For gap-created items (which start from the RateCard base), rc.Apply is called Quantity-1 times because the RateCard already represents one application.

patterns.name: Relative cadence via ISODuration offsets
patterns.description: setItemRelativeCadence converts absolute timestamps to ISO 8601 durations relative to phase start using datetime.ISODurationBetween, stored as ActiveFromOverrideRelativeToPhaseStart / ActiveToOverrideRelativeToPhaseStart. Never store absolute times in item cadence overrides.
patterns.example:
diff := datetime.ISODurationBetween(phaseCadence.ActiveFrom, *target.From)
item.ActiveFromOverrideRelativeToPhaseStart = &diff

patterns.name: Restore merges adjacent equal items
patterns.description: restore.go undoes addon effects per-item via AddonRateCard.Restore, deletes items whose zeroRateCardCheck.CanDelete() returns true, then merges consecutive items with equal RateCard + Annotations + BillingBehaviorOverride whose cadences are adjacent.
patterns.example:
canMerge := targetItem.RateCard.Equal(testItem.RateCard) &&
    reflect.DeepEqual(targetItem.Annotations, testItem.Annotations) &&
    targetCadence.ActiveTo.Equal(testCadence.ActiveFrom)

patterns.name: Zero rate-card deletion guard
patterns.description: zeroRateCardCheck in zeroratecard.go determines if a restored item can be deleted by inspecting FlatPrice amount and EntitlementTemplate type/value and OwnerSubSystem annotations. Only if all checks pass is the item safe to remove.
patterns.example:
chk := zeroRateCardCheck{itemAnnotations: item.Annotations, rc: target}
if chk.CanDelete() { rmIdxs = append(rmIdxs, idx) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Diffable interface and someDiffable concrete helper. The two-method contract (GetApplies/GetRestores) is the only public API shape this package exports.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add I/O here; Diffable is a pure value type with no side-effects.

key_file_guides.role: GetDiffableFromAddon converts a SubscriptionAddon into a Diffable by mapping instances to per-instance diffables aggregated via NewAggregateAppliesToSpec.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns nil, nil when addon has no instances — callers must check for nil Diffable before calling spec.Apply.

key_file_guides.role: Core apply algorithm: iterates phases in addon cadence range, manages gaps, splits items, applies rc.Apply() the correct number of times.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Quantity application count differs for existing items (Quantity) vs gap-created items (Quantity-1). Mixing these breaks quantity correctness.

key_file_guides.role: Inverse of apply: calls AddonRateCard.Restore per item, removes zero items, merges adjacent equal fragments.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Merge logic uses reflect.DeepEqual on Annotations and BillingBehaviorOverride — pointer fields must be comparable via DeepEqual.

key_file_guides.role: Predicate for whether a restored RateCard is effectively empty and safe to delete. Only inspects Price and EntitlementTemplate fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only measures fields that AddonRateCard.Apply/Restore touches — adding checks for other fields here risks false positives.

key_file_guides.role: GetAffectedItemIDs computes a map[rateCardKey][]itemID used by the HTTP layer to populate AffectedSubscriptionItemIds in API responses.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Has a FIXME noting it belongs elsewhere; do not add business logic here.

anti_patterns: Adding database or service calls inside Diffable.GetApplies / GetRestores — this package is a pure in-memory transform layer
Calling spec.Apply with a nil Diffable (GetDiffableFromAddon returns nil when no instances exist)
Using absolute timestamps in SubscriptionItemSpec cadence fields instead of ISODuration offsets relative to phase start
Modifying phase.ItemsByKey in-place without rebuilding the slice — restore.go always rebuilds filteredItems and mergedItems slices
Assuming Apply then Restore is a no-op for all items — only items fully within the addon cadence with zero restored value are deleted

decisions.decision: Pure in-memory spec mutation with no I/O
decisions.rationale: The diff/restore operations must be composable and testable without DB; callers (workflow service) own the persistence boundary.

decisions.decision: Gap-based insertion rather than full spec rebuild
decisions.rationale: Preserves existing item splits from prior edits and only touches the time range covered by the addon cadence.

code_examples.scenario: Apply addon to spec and later restore it

openmeter/subscription/addon/http.purpose: HTTP handler layer for subscription addon CRUD, bridging the v1 API to subscriptionaddon.Service and subscriptionworkflow.Service using the standard httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Request,Response,Params] pipeline.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs three-stage pipeline
patterns.description: Each operation uses httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoder, operation, encoder, ...options). The decoder resolves namespace and decodes body, the operation calls domain services, the encoder writes JSON with a fixed status code.

patterns.name: Upsert-on-create convenience
patterns.description: CreateSubscriptionAddon checks if the addon is already attached to the subscription and routes to ChangeAddonQuantity instead of AddAddon, providing idempotent create semantics. Both paths return HTTP 200.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via NamespaceDecoder
patterns.description: All decoders call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) which delegates to h.NamespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx). Missing namespace returns HTTP 500 via commonhttp.NewHTTPError.
patterns.example:
ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: Response always requires SubscriptionView
patterns.description: MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse needs both the SubscriptionAddon and its parent SubscriptionView to compute AffectedSubscriptionItemIds via addondiff.GetAffectedItemIDs. Every Get/List endpoint fetches the view separately.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface, HandlerConfig (three services + NamespaceDecoder + Logger), NewHandler constructor, and resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HandlerConfig embeds all three services (SubscriptionAddonService, SubscriptionWorkflowService, SubscriptionService) — all are required; missing any causes panics at call time.

key_file_guides.role: MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse builds api.SubscriptionAddon including AffectedSubscriptionItemIds. MapCreateSubscriptionAddonRequestToInput translates API body to workflow input.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns error if addon has no instances (empty union of periods). Calls addondiff.GetAffectedItemIDs which needs the full SubscriptionView.

key_file_guides.role: Upsert-on-create: if addon already attached, calls ChangeAddonQuantity; otherwise AddAddon. Both paths return HTTP 200.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No 201 distinction between create and update paths — intentional for idempotency.

key_file_guides.role: PATCH-style quantity update. Requires both Timing and Quantity fields; returns 400 validation error otherwise.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Timing and Quantity nil-checks are manual in the decoder, not via model.Validate — keep them in sync with the API schema.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic to decoder functions — decoders only parse and validate shape, not semantics
Forgetting to fetch SubscriptionView before calling MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse — the function requires it to compute AffectedSubscriptionItemIds
Using httptransport.NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs when URL path params (SubscriptionID, SubscriptionAddonID) are needed
Returning nil SubscriptionView to MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse — it will error on instance union computation

decisions.rationale: API consumers often want idempotent addon attachment; routing to ChangeAddonQuantity when the addon is already present avoids a 409 conflict and simplifies client logic.

code_examples.scenario: Standard handler wiring with URL params and namespace resolution

openmeter/subscription/addon/repo.purpose: Ent-backed PostgreSQL repository for subscription addons and their quantity timeline, implementing entutils.TransactingRepo so all mutations participate in caller-supplied transactions. Quantities are append-only.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping all mutations
patterns.description: Every method body is wrapped in entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, r, func(ctx, repo) ...) so the repo rebinds to the transaction already in ctx, or starts its own via Self(). Never call repo.db directly inside a method body.

patterns.name: Tx/Self/WithTx transaction trinity
patterns.description: Each repo struct implements Tx() (hijack via HijackTx + NewTxDriver), Self() (identity), and WithTx() (rebind to tx client via NewTxClientFromRawConfig). Both subscriptionAddonRepo and subscriptionAddonQuantityRepo have their own block in transaction.go.

patterns.name: Eager-load via querySubscriptionAddon helper
patterns.description: All Get/List queries go through querySubscriptionAddon() which loads WithAddon (including Ratecards->Features, Ratecards->TaxCode) and WithQuantities ordered by ActiveFrom asc, CreatedAt asc. Never add a bare db.SubscriptionAddon.Query() without the helper.

patterns.name: Paginate-or-return-all for List
patterns.description: List returns all results without pagination when filter.Page.IsZero(); otherwise uses Ent's Paginate helper and entutils.MapPagedWithErr.

patterns.name: Quantities are append-only
patterns.description: SubscriptionAddonQuantityRepository has only Create — no Update or Delete. The quantity timeline is an audit-safe ledger; changes are new records with a later ActiveFrom.
patterns.example: // Only Create is defined — no Update/Delete methods exist on subscriptionAddonQuantityRepo

key_file_guides.role: Create, Get, List for SubscriptionAddon. Get wraps db.IsNotFound in models.NewGenericNotFoundError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All queries must use querySubscriptionAddon() — bare queries miss eager-loaded RateCard/Quantity edges.

key_file_guides.role: Append-only Create for SubscriptionAddonQuantity. Namespace comes from parent subscriptionAddonID.Namespace.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No Update or Delete methods exist by design — do not add them.

key_file_guides.role: MapSubscriptionAddon and MapSubscriptionAddons convert db rows to domain types, delegating RateCard mapping to addonrepo.FromAddonRateCardRow.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MapSubscriptionAddon returns error if Edges.Addon is nil for rate card mapping — the edge must always be loaded via querySubscriptionAddon.

key_file_guides.role: Tx/Self/WithTx implementations for both subscriptionAddonRepo and subscriptionAddonQuantityRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both repos use db.HijackTx independently — if a third repo type is added here, it needs its own Tx/Self/WithTx block.

anti_patterns: Calling repo.db methods directly inside Create/Get/List without TransactingRepo wrapping — bypasses ctx-bound transaction
Fetching SubscriptionAddon without querySubscriptionAddon() helper — misses eager-loaded RateCard/Quantity edges
Updating or deleting SubscriptionAddonQuantity rows — quantities are immutable append-only records
Using a raw db.Client in WithTx instead of db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig

decisions.rationale: The quantity timeline is an audit-safe ledger; changes are represented by new quantity records with a later ActiveFrom rather than modifying existing rows.

code_examples.scenario: Create subscription addon inside a transaction

openmeter/subscription/addon/service.purpose: Business logic service for subscription addons: validates plan compatibility, instance-type constraints, and MaxQuantity limits before persisting via SubscriptionAddonRepository and publishing Watermill events. Implements subscriptionaddon.Service.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() and fail-fast construction
patterns.description: Constructor NewService accepts a Config struct and calls cfg.Validate() before returning — fail-fast at wiring time rather than runtime nil-pointer panics.

patterns.name: Pre-persist validation chain
patterns.description: Create and ChangeQuantity both: (1) call input.Validate(), (2) fetch the addon to check InstanceType, (3) fetch the subscription view to check PlanRef and current phase, (4) fetch PlanAddon for FromPlanPhase and MaxQuantity. Only then enter transaction.Run.

patterns.name: transaction.Run for multi-step writes
patterns.description: All writes use transaction.Run(ctx, s.cfg.TxManager, func) which coordinates the repo Tx/Self/WithTx chain. Repo calls inside the transaction func use the tx-bound ctx.
patterns.example:
return transaction.Run(ctx, s.cfg.TxManager, func(ctx context.Context) (*subscriptionaddon.SubscriptionAddon, error) {
	repo.Create(ctx, ns, ...)
	qtyRepo.Create(ctx, id, ...)
	add, _ := repo.Get(ctx, id)
	s.cfg.Publisher.Publish(ctx, subscriptionaddon.NewCreatedEvent(...))
	return add, nil
})

patterns.name: Event publish inside transaction
patterns.description: After persisting, Publisher.Publish is called inside the transaction.Run func so the event is tied to the same unit of work. NewCreatedEvent / NewChangeQuantityEvent are factory functions in the subscriptionaddon package.
patterns.example: s.cfg.Publisher.Publish(ctx, subscriptionaddon.NewCreatedEvent(ctx, sView.Customer, *subscriptionAddon))

patterns.name: Phase-ordered compatibility check
patterns.description: To verify an addon can be added at a given time, the service iterates sView.Phases in order and checks whether compatibility.FromPlanPhase is reached before the phase containing the addon start time.

key_file_guides.role: Full implementation of subscriptionaddon.Service: Create, Get, List, ChangeQuantity with all validation and transaction coordination.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Create and ChangeQuantity duplicate several validation steps (InstanceType, FromPlanPhase, MaxQuantity) — keep them in sync if validation rules change. Addons cannot be added to custom subscriptions (no PlanRef).

anti_patterns: Calling SubAddRepo or SubAddQtyRepo outside of transaction.Run for writes — partial writes will occur on failure
Bypassing cfg.Validate() by constructing the service struct directly instead of via NewService
Publishing events outside the transaction.Run func — event and DB write will be inconsistent on rollback
Adding addons to subscriptions without a PlanRef — the service explicitly rejects custom subscriptions

decisions.decision: Service validates plan compatibility before any DB write
decisions.rationale: Addon purchase must respect plan-level constraints (FromPlanPhase, MaxQuantity, InstanceType) that live in the product catalog, not in the subscription tables.
Quantity timeline is append-only and models a different entity; a dedicated repo keeps quantity writes isolated and avoids loading all quantities on every addon mutation.

code_examples.scenario: Create a subscription addon with validation and transactional persistence
